mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation signals sustainable capacity utilization that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during low-inflation periods do not precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, with mean forward return below zero and t-statistic less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.0 percent triggers risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, invalidating the risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak consumer demand that drags down S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with the interaction effect showing positive or null predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during high unemployment periods signals severe output contraction that forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high unemployment periods do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, with mean forward return exceeding zero and failing significance tests.
